intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inconsistent with visual evidence. The expected specimens (P1-P4) are not visible as healthy, established plants. The scene is dominated by soil, debris, and a singular white anchor object.

inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected; only the white rabbit anchor remains.

plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; no Pothos foliage.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, moist, and appears undisturbed across all pots.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Presence of a spoon in the earliest image, replaced by a white cylindrical object (likely a sensor or container) in subsequent frames.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-5: Removal of the spoon from the desk surface.
temporal_deltas.t-5_to_current: Introduction and stabilization of a white cylindrical object near the yellow pot. No vegetative growth observed throughout the sequence.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable for the registered species. The absence of chlorophyll-bearing tissue suggests either total plant failure or that the pots were never successfully populated with the registered specimens.
anomalies: Presence of a white cylindrical object (New Intervention).
Absence of all registered plant life (Systemic Loss).
White rabbit anchor is the only biological/structural constant.
vision_report.narrative_description: My audit confirms a state of total botanical vacancy. The 'Garden' currently functions as a soil-based staging area rather than a living collection. The white rabbit anchor in P3 is the only non-soil element present. The environment appears static, with no signs of germination, growth, or decay, as the plants were likely absent or already lost at the start of this observation window.
